Little Mermaid Statue

This iconic statue is one of Copenhagen's most famous tourist attractions. It's based on the character from Grimm's Fairy Tales, and it sits on a rock by the waterside at the Langelinie promenade. It's a must-visit spot for literature and art lovers, and the best part is, it's free to visit and open all week.
Rundetaarn
is an iconic symbol of Copenhagen's rich history. It was built in the 17th century during the reign of King Christian IV and is the oldest functioning observatory in Europe
It's a world-renowned restaurant, known for its innovative and artistic approach to cooking. The restaurant has been voted as the best restaurant in the world multiple times

This palace is one of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically. It houses the Danish Parliament, the Supreme Court, and the Ministry of State. Parts of the palace are used by the Royal Family for various functions and events.

text: he official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euros.
There are in total 6 metros in Copenhagen. Visitors can reach anywhere by metro.

The official language is Danish, but English is widely spoken in Copenhagen.
